children.heading: Factors considered; revision to reflect standards
children.label: a Factors considered; revision to reflect standards
A
children.text: the number of eligible Indian students served and total student population of the school;

children.label: i
children.text: the isolation of the school;

children.label: ii
children.text: the need for special staffing, transportation, or educational programs;

children.label: iii
children.text: food and housing costs;

children.label: iv
children.text: maintenance and repair costs associated with the physical condition of the educational facilities;

children.label: v
children.text: special transportation and other costs of isolated and small schools;

children.label: vi
children.text: the costs of home-living (dormitory) arrangements, where determined necessary by a tribal governing body or designated school board;

children.label: vii
children.text: costs associated with greater lengths of service by education personnel;

children.label: viii
children.text: the costs of therapeutic programs for students requiring such programs; and

children.label: ix
children.text: special costs for gifted and talented students;

children.label: C
children.text: the cost of providing academic services which are at least equivalent to those provided by public schools in the State in which the school is located;

children.label: D
children.text: whether the available funding will enable the school involved to comply with the accreditation standards applicable to the school under ; andsection 2001 of this title

children.label: E
children.text: such other relevant factors as the Secretary determines are appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Upon the establishment of the standards required in , the Secretary shall revise the formula established under this subsection to reflect the cost of funding such standards.section 2002 of this title

children.heading: Review of formula
children.label: B Review of formula
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall review the formula established under this section and shall take such steps as are necessary to increase the availability of counseling and therapeutic programs for students in off-reservation home-living (dormitory) schools and other Bureau-operated residential facilities.January 1, 2003

children.heading: Review of standards
children.label: C Review of standards
children.text: Concurrent with such action, the Secretary shall review the standards established under to be certain that adequate provision is made for parental notification regarding, and consent for, such counseling and therapeutic programs.section 2002 of this title

children.heading: Pro rata allotment
children.label: b Pro rata allotment
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, Federal funds appropriated for the general local operation of Bureau-funded schools shall be allotted pro rata in accordance with the formula established under subsection (a).

children.heading: Annual adjustment; reservation of amount for school board activities
children.label: c Annual adjustment; reservation of amount for school board activities
A
children.text: Uses a weighted unit of 1.2 for each eligible Indian student enrolled in the seventh and eighth grades of the school in considering the number of eligible Indian students served by the school.

children.label: B
children.text: Considers a school with an enrollment of less than 50 eligible Indian students as having an average daily attendance of 50 eligible Indian students for purposes of implementing the adjustment factor for small schools.

children.label: C
children.text: Takes into account the provision of residential services on less than a 9-month basis at a school when the school board and supervisor of the school determine that a less than 9-month basis will be implemented for the school year involved.

children.label: i
children.text: is gifted and talented; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is enrolled in the school on a full-time basis,

children.label: i
children.text: the certification of the Indian or Native language curriculum by the school board of such school to the Secretary, together with an estimate of the number of full-time students expected to be enrolled in the curriculum in the second school year for which the certification is made; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the funds appropriated for allotment under this section are designated by the appropriations Act appropriating such funds as the amount necessary to implement such adjustment at such school without reducing allotments made under this section to any school by virtue of such adjustment.

children.label: II
children.text: 1 percent of such allotted funds,

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Each local school board, and any agency school board that serves as a local school board for any grant or contract school, shall ensure that each individual who is a new member of the school board receives, within 1 year after the individual becomes a member of the school board, 40 hours of training relevant to that individual’s service on the board.

children.heading: Types of training
children.label: ii Types of training
children.text: Such training may include training concerning legal issues pertaining to Bureau-funded schools, legal issues pertaining to school boards, ethics, and other topics determined to be appropriate by the school board.

children.heading: Recommendation
children.label: iii Recommendation
children.text: The training described in this subparagraph shall not be required, but is recommended, for a tribal governing body that serves in the capacity of a school board.

children.heading: Reservation of amount for emergencies
children.label: d Reservation of amount for emergencies

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall reserve from the funds available for distribution for each fiscal year under this section an amount that, in the aggregate, equals 1 percent of the funds available for such purpose for that fiscal year, to be used, at the discretion of the Director of the Office of Indian Education Programs, to meet emergencies and unforeseen contingencies affecting the education programs funded under this section.

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: 2 Use of funds
children.text: Funds reserved under this subsection may be expended only for education services or programs, including emergency repairs of educational facilities, at a schoolsite (as defined by section 2503(c)(2) of this title).1So in original. Probably should be section “2502(c)(2)”.1

children.heading: Availability of funds
children.label: 3 Availability of funds
children.text: Funds reserved under this subsection shall remain available without fiscal year limitation until expended. However, the aggregate amount available from all fiscal years may not exceed 1 percent of the current year funds.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 4 Report
children.text: When the Secretary makes funds available under this subsection, the Secretary shall report such action to the appropriate committees of Congress within the annual budget submission.

children.heading: Supplemental appropriations
children.label: e Supplemental appropriations
children.text: Supplemental appropriations enacted to meet increased pay costs attributable to school level personnel shall be distributed under this section.

is a member of, or is at least one-fourth degree Indian blood descendant of a member of, a tribe that is eligible for the special programs and services provided by the United States through the Bureau to Indians because of their status as Indians;
resides on or near a reservation or meets the criteria for attendance at a Bureau off-reservation home-living school; and
is enrolled in a Bureau-funded school.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No eligible Indian student or a student attending a Bureau school under paragraph (2)(C) may be charged tuition for attendance at a Bureau school or contract or grant school.

children.heading: Attendance of non-Indian students at Bureau schools
children.label: 2 Attendance of non-Indian students at Bureau schools
A
children.text: the Secretary determines that the student’s attendance will not adversely affect the school’s program for eligible Indian students because of cost, overcrowding, or violation of standards or accreditation;

children.label: B
children.text: the school board consents;

children.label: C
children.text: the student is a dependent of a Bureau, Indian Health Service, or tribal government employee who lives on or near the school site; or

children.label: D
children.text: tuition is paid for the student that is not more than the tuition charged by the nearest public school district for out-of-district students and shall be in addition to the school’s allocation under this section.

children.heading: Attendance of non-Indian students at contract and grant schools
children.label: 3 Attendance of non-Indian students at contract and grant schools
children.text: The school board of a contract or grant school may permit students who are not eligible Indian students under this subsection to attend its contract school or grant school. Any tuition collected for those students shall be in addition to funding received under this section.

children.heading: Funds available without fiscal year limitation
children.label: h Funds available without fiscal year limitation
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, at the election of the school board of a Bureau school made at any time during the fiscal year, a portion equal to not more than 15 percent of the funds allocated with respect to a school under this section for any fiscal year shall remain available to the school for expenditure without fiscal year limitation. The Assistant Secretary shall take such steps as are necessary to implement this subsection.

children.heading: Students at Richfield dormitory, Richfield, Utah
children.label: i Students at Richfield dormitory, Richfield, Utah

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Tuition for the instruction of each out-of-State Indian student in a home-living situation at the Richfield dormitory in Richfield, Utah, who attends Sevier County high schools in Richfield, Utah, for an academic year, shall be paid from Indian school equalization program funds authorized in this section and section 2009 of this title, at a rate not to exceed the weighted amount provided for under subsection (b) for a student for that year.2

children.heading: No administrative cost funds
children.label: 2 No administrative cost funds
children.text: No additional administrative cost funds shall be provided under this chapter to pay for administrative costs relating to the instruction of the students.
